prompt: WSL keeps eating like 90% of my 32GB laptop, so I'm about to set memory=6GB in .wslconfig so vmmemWSL physically can't grow. Good idea? My daily setup inside WSL: Claude Code, vite dev server, pyright, and a few MCP servers. Current config has swap=0.
assertions: Warns that lowering the cap alone with swap=0 will stall/freeze at the new boundary rather than fix pressure
Recommends adding swap before (or together with) lowering the memory cap
Recommends reducing resident helpers (stale/duplicate node, pyright, MCP workers) as part of the fix
Grounds advice in WSL2 VM behavior (vmmemWSL envelope / MemAvailable), not generic tips
